volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 88b - Section 161b)

sections.label: Section 88b
sections.translation: And did you not lose your crow , when you fell sprawling on the ground ? Tell me that .

sections.label: Section 90
sections.translation: Not at all .

sections.label: Section 90b
sections.translation: Where is it , then ?

sections.label: Section 90c
sections.translation: It flew away .

sections.label: Section 91
sections.original: οὐκ ἆρʼ ἀφῆκας ; ὦγάθʼ ὡς ἀνδρεῖος εἶ .
sections.translation: And you did not let it go ? Oh ! you brave fellow !

sections.label: Section 92
sections.original: ἄνοιγε τὴν ὕλην , ἵνʼ ἐξέλθω ποτέ .
sections.translation: Open the thicket , that I may go out !

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: ὦ Ἡράκλεις τουτὶ τί ποτʼ ἐστὶ τὸ θηρίον ;
sections.translation: By Heracles ! what a creature ! what plumage ! What means this triple crest ?

sections.label: Section 95
sections.translation: Who wants me ?

sections.label: Section 95b
sections.translation: The twelve great gods have used you ill , it seems .

sections.label: Section 96b
sections.translation: Are you twitting me about my feathers ? I have been a man , strangers .

sections.label: Section 98b
sections.translation: It ' s not you we are jeering at .

sections.label: Section 98c
sections.translation: At what , then ?

sections.label: Section 99
sections.original: τὸ ῥάμφος ἡμῖν σου γέλοιον φαίνεται .
sections.translation: Why , it ' s your beak that looks so ridiculous to us .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.translation: This is how Sophocles outrages me in his tragedies . Know , I once was Tereus .

sections.label: Section 102
sections.original: Τηρεὺς γὰρ εἶ σύ ; πότερον ὄρνις ἢ ταὧς ;
sections.translation: You were Tereus , and what are you now ? a bird or a peacock ?

sections.label: Section 103
sections.translation: I am a bird .

sections.label: Section 103b
sections.translation: Then where are your feathers ? I don ' t see any .

sections.label: Section 104
sections.translation: They have fallen off .

sections.label: Section 104b
sections.translation: Through illness ?

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ τὸν χειμῶνα πάντα τὤρνεα
sections.translation: No . All birds moult their feathers , you know , every winter , and others grow in their place . But tell me , who are you ?

sections.label: Section 107b
sections.translation: We ? We are mortals .

sections.label: Section 108
sections.translation: From what country ?

sections.label: Section 108b
sections.translation: From the land of the beautiful galleys .

sections.label: Section 109
sections.translation: Are you dicasts ?

sections.label: Section 109b
sections.translation: No , if anything ,

sections.label: Section 110
sections.translation: we are anti-dicasts .

sections.label: Section 110b
sections.translation: Is that kind of seed sown among you ?

sections.label: Section 111b
sections.original: ὀλίγον ζητῶν ἂν ἐξ ἀγροῦ λάβοις .
sections.translation: You have to look hard to find even a little in our fields .

sections.label: Section 112
sections.original: πράγους δὲ δὴ τοῦ δεομένω δεῦρʼ ἤλθετον ;
sections.translation: What brings you here ?

sections.label: Section 113
sections.translation: We wish to pay you a visit .

sections.label: Section 114
sections.translation: What for ?

sections.label: Section 114b
sections.original: ὅτι πρῶτα μὲν ἦσθʼ ἄνθρωπος ὥσπερ νὼ ποτέ ,
sections.translation: Because you formerly were a man , like we are ,

sections.label: Section 115
sections.translation: formerly you had debts , as we have , formerly you did not want to pay them , like ourselves ; furthermore , being turned into a bird , you have when flying seen all lands and seas . Thus you have all human knowledge as well as that of birds .

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: ταῦτ οὖν ἱκέται νὼ πρὸς σὲ δεῦρʼ ἀφίγμεθα ,
sections.translation: And hence we have come to you to beg you to direct us to some cosy town , in which one can repose as if on thick coverlets .

sections.label: Section 123
sections.original: ἔμειτα μείζω τῶν Κραναῶν ζητεῖς πόλιν ;
sections.translation: And are you looking for a greater city than Athens ?

sections.label: Section 124
sections.original: μείζω μὲν οὐδέν , προσφορωτέραν δὲ νῷν .
sections.translation: No , not a greater , but one more pleasant to live in .

sections.label: Section 125
sections.translation: Then you are looking for an aristocratic country .

sections.label: Section 125b
sections.translation: I ? Not at all ! I hold the son of Scellias in horror .

sections.label: Section 127
sections.original: ποίαν τινʼ οὖν ἥδιστʼ ἂν οἰκοῖτʼ ἂν πόλιν ;
sections.translation: But , after all , what sort of city would please you best ?

sections.label: Section 128
sections.original: ὅπου τὰ μέγιστα πράγματʼ εἴη τοιάδε ·
sections.translation: A place where the following would be the most important business transacted . — Some friend would come knocking at the door quite early in the morning

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: λέγοι ταδί · πρὸς τοῦ Διὸς τοὐλυμπίου
sections.translation: saying , By Olympian Zeus , be at my house early , as soon as you have bathed , and bring your children too . I am giving a nuptial feast , so don ' t fail , or else don ' t cross my threshold when I am in distress .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: νὴ Δία ταλαιπώρων γε πραγμάτων ἐρᾷς .
sections.translation: Ah ! that ' s what may be called being fond of hardships ! To Pisthetaerus . And what say you ?

sections.label: Section 136b
sections.translation: My tastes are similar .

sections.label: Section 136c
sections.translation: And they are ?

sections.label: Section 137
sections.original: ὅπου ξυναντῶν μοι ταδί τις μέμψεται
sections.translation: I want a town where the father of a handsome lad will stop in the street and say to me reproachfully as if I had failed him , Ah ! Is this well done , Stilbonides ?

sections.label: Section 140
sections.translation: You met my son coming from the bath after the gymnasium and you neither spoke to him , nor kissed him , nor took him with you , nor ever once felt his balls . Would anyone call you an old friend of mine ?

sections.label: Section 143
sections.original: ὦ δειλακρίων σὺ τῶν κακῶν οἵων ἐρᾷς .
sections.translation: Ah ! wag , I see you are fond of suffering . But there is a city of delights such as you want .

sections.label: Section 145
sections.translation: It ' s on the Red Sea .

sections.label: Section 145b
sections.translation: Oh , no . Not a sea-port , where some fine morning the Salaminian galley can appear , bringing a process-server along . Have you no Greek town you can propose to us ?

sections.label: Section 149
sections.original: τί οὐ τὸν Ἠλεῖον Λέπρεον οἰκίζετον
sections.translation: Why not choose Lepreum in Elis for your settlement ?

sections.label: Section 150b
sections.original: ὁτιὴ νὴ τοὺς θεοὺς ὅσʼ οὐκ ἰδὼν
sections.translation: By Zeus ! I could not look at Lepreum without disgust , because of Melanthius .

sections.label: Section 152
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰσὶν ἕτεροι τῆς Λοκρίδος Ὀπούντιοι ,
sections.translation: Then , again , there is the Opuntian Locris , where you could live .

sections.label: Section 153b
sections.translation: I would not be Opuntian for a talent .

sections.label: Section 155
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ δὴ τίς ἔσθʼ ὁ μετʼ ὀρνίθων βίος ;
sections.translation: But come , what is it like to live with the birds ? You should know pretty well .

sections.label: Section 156b
sections.translation: Why , it ' s not a disagreeable life . In the first place , one has no purse .

sections.label: Section 158
sections.original: πολλήν γʼ ἀφεῖλες τοῦ βίου κιβδηλίαν .
sections.translation: That does away with a lot of roguery .

sections.label: Section 159
sections.original: νεμόμεσθα δʼ ἐν κήποις τὰ λευκὰ σήσαμα
sections.translation: For food the gardens yield us white sesame ,

sections.label: Section 160
sections.original: καὶ μύρτα καὶ μήκωνα καὶ σισύμβρια .
sections.translation: myrtle-berries , poppies and mint .

sections.label: Section 161
sections.original: ὑμεῖς μὲν ἆρα ζῆτε νυμφίων βίον .
sections.translation: Why , ' tis the life of the newly-wed indeed .

sections.label: Section 161b
sections.translation: Ha ! I am beginning to see a great plan , which will transfer the supreme power to the birds , if you will but take my advice .
